---
title: Data & models version control with DVC
author: Marie-Hélène Burle
---

:::{.def}

[Data version control (DVC)](https://dvc.org/) is an open source tool that brings all the versioning and collaboration capabilities you use on your code with Git to your data and machine learning workflow.

If you use datasets in your work, it makes it easy to track their evolution.

If you are in the field of machine learning, it additionally allows you to track your models, manage your pipelines from parameters to metrics, collaborate on your experiments, and integrate with the continuous integration tool for machine learning projects [CML](https://cml.dev/).

This webinar will show you how to get started with DVC, first in the simple case where you just want to put your data under version control, then in the more complex situation where you want to manage your machine learning workflow in a more organized and reproducible fashion.

:::

[Slides](wb_dvc_slides.qmd){.btn .btn-outline-primary} [(Click and wait: this reveal.js presentation may take a little time to load.)]{.inlinenote}
<br><br>

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/embed/2MQVF78FRKs >}}

<br>
*[Slides content](wb_dvc_content.qmd) for easier browsing.*

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "LearningResource",
  "name": "Data & models version control with DVC",
  "description": "Data version control (DVC) is an open source tool that brings all the versioning and collaboration capabilities you use on your code with Git to your data and machine learning workflow.\n\nIf you use datasets in your work, it makes it easy to track their evolution.\n\nIf you are in the field of machine learning, it additionally allows you to track your models, manage your pipelines from parameters to metrics, collaborate on your experiments, and integrate with the continuous integration tool for machine learning projects CML.\n\nThis webinar will show you how to get started with DVC, first in the simple case where you just want to put your data under version control, then in the more complex situation where you want to manage your machine learning workflow in a more organized and reproducible fashion.",
  "url": "https://mint.westdri.ca/tools/wb_typst",
  "learningResourceType": "slides, video",
  "inLanguage": "en",
  "license": "https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/",
  "provider": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "SFU Research Computing Group",
  },
  "keywords": "DVC, Git, version control, AI, deep learning, data, models",
  "author": {
    "@type": "Person",
    "name": "Marie-Hélène Burle"
  }
}
</script>
